task_completion.description: Open the Landsat multi-spectral image in ESA SNAP Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 'landsat_multispectral.tif' is visible in the Product Explorer panel within the ESA SNAP Desktop interface.
The Mask Manager or a mask creation dialog is open, showing a new mask explicitly named 'veg_mask'.

task_completion.description: Set the mask expression to 'band_2 > band_3'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The expression editor or mask definition field shows the exact logical expression 'band_2 > band_3' for the 'veg_mask'.

task_completion.description: Compute statistics for all four bands using the mask as an ROI constraint
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Statistics tool window is open with all four spectral bands selected, and 'veg_mask' is checked or selected in the ROI/Mask constraint section.

task_completion.description: Export the computed statistics to a text file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or export window is visible, saving the statistics report to the path '/home/ga/snap_exports/veg_statistics.txt'.

task_completion.description: Save the modified product in BEAM-DIMAP format
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Product As' dialog or similar export window shows the product being saved to '/home/ga/snap_exports/masked_landsat.dim' with the BEAM-DIMAP format selected.

integrity.description: Agent used the ESA SNAP Desktop GUI for the workflow
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the SNAP graphical interface (menus, dialogs, Mask Manager, Statistics tool) rather than terminal commands or Python scripts.

integrity.description: Statistics were genuinely computed by the software
integrity.visual_evidence: The SNAP Statistics window displays actual computed values, histograms, or progress bars, proving the tool was run, rather than the agent manually creating a text file.

integrity.description: Agent used the application's save dialogs to export files
integrity.visual_evidence: File paths for the exports are entered into SNAP's native save/export dialog boxes, not created via terminal echo or text editors.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input file is located at /home/ga/snap_data/landsat_multispectral.tif. The mask must be named 'veg_mask' with the exact expression 'band_2 > band_3'. The statistics report must be exported to /home/ga/snap_exports/veg_statistics.txt. The final product must be saved as a BEAM-DIMAP file to /home/ga/snap_exports/masked_landsat.dim.
